name: Greek entity theia
shortDescription: Theia is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology, primarily associated with sight, light, and the brilliance of the blue sky. She is the mother of Helios (the Sun), Selene (the Moon), and Eos (the Dawn).
description: Theia, also known as Euryphaessa, is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology. She is the daughter of Gaia (Earth) and Uranus (Sky), and one of the twelve Titans. Theia's primary domain is that of sight, brilliance, and the ethereal light that permeates the sky. She is often described as the "divine" or "brilliant" one, embodying the radiant beauty of the heavens. Theia is primarily known for being the consort of Hyperion, another Titan, and the mother of three important celestial deities: Helios (the Sun), Selene (the Moon), and Eos (the Dawn). Through her children, Theia plays a crucial role in the cyclical passage of time and the natural order of the cosmos. She is a powerful symbol of the primordial forces that shaped the world, representing the illuminating and clarifying aspects of the universe. While Theia does not feature prominently in many well-known myths, her role as the mother of Helios, Selene, and Eos firmly establishes her significance within the Greek pantheon as a vital element in the celestial order. She represents the divine radiance and the power of sight, essential for understanding the world around us. Her association with Hyperion, the Titan of light, reinforces her connection to the celestial realm and the primordial forces that govern the cosmos. Theia's name itself suggests vision and clarity, further solidifying her role as a goddess of light and perception.
